rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MILTON COHN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #11, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/12/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Why is Margaret Gilbert's address shown in the second amended petition to be in c/o Leo Gilbert in Kirkland, WA? But notice was mailed to her in Seattle? Notice must be given directly to a person entitled to notice, unless the person directs in writing that notice be mailed in care of another. [CRC 7.51(a)(2).] (Deceased beneficiaries do not need to be listed among "current beneficiaries."). (2) The second amended petition does not set forth what the amount of bond should now be. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/28/25. (Please do NOT file another fully amended petition, especially if not all allegations are going to be updated, such as paragraph 12 of this second amended petition, when the petitioner's declaration was filed on 10/18/24, not "concurrently with this petition." A supplement or an amendment to the second amended petition (and a new proof of service, if applicable) should suffice to address these remaining issues.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WIGDBERTO MAGOBET
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/12/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of notice has been filed. (Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.]) (3) Has a written agreement been filed for the record, evidencing the heirs' agreement re treatment of the real property? (4) If there is cash in the estate (paragraph 19), why has no I&A been filed yet to report it? Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/28/25. (The administrator was appointed on 5/25/22. The order was not filed until 5/31/22.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

Luckett) have a right to know why the estate is not ready to be closed and distributed to them, when the surplus funds from the foreclosure sale were deposited with the Court on 7/31/24. rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF LARRY MAH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/9/2025 for further review of the amended petition and fee requests. The following information needs to be supplemented one week before the next hearing. (1) Provide the purpose and payee for the $650 disbursement made on 6/23/23 (under the Medical category, #1020 "2030"). (2) Provide the purpose and payee for the $275 payment made on 5/24/24 (under Maintenance & Repair, #1144 "$95"). (3) Clarify whether Gordon Fong and Brendan Wong are the caregivers/private cleaners referenced in the CI report. (4) Explain the car towing and inspection fee of $1,400 made on September 8, 2023, to Weiping Zhang, considering that I&A does not report any car. (5) Provide further allegations regarding whether the $400 monthly allowance to the conservatee should continue without being accounted for. (6) Submit the Thrifty Savings Plan bank statement showing the beginning balance, which has not been filed. (7) Provide further allegations outlining the regular services rendered during this first account period and to be performed during the next account period for the court to consider granting the monthly compensation payment to the conservator to continue. Prob. Code sec. 2643.1. (8) Provide supporting allegations regarding the increase of the conservator's hourly rate ($185), as the initial petition had indicated $145. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HARUWN K. WESLEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/7/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The cash proceeds from Athene Annuity & Life were reported on the I&A as $11,563.69. How is the amount now $15,000? (4) A petition for approval of an account or a report accompanying the petition shall contain all the statements required by Probate Code Sect. 1064(a). How was cash invested? A proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/23/25. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee WAS appointed. Please read the entire sentence.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PETER Q. YANG TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Trust Administration And Appointment Of Yijun Yang As Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/12/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) The proof of service is incomplete at item 4a (date mailed). (3) Two of the consents to the petition were signed by agents ostensibly acting under powers of attorney. Please file a copy of each durable power of attorney for Rose Yang Chow and Helen Yang. (4) Rose Yang Chow's consent shows her address as 801 W. Commonwealth Ave., etc., but the amendment shows her address as 7769 E. Viewrim Dr., etc. Did she move after the petition was filed (after her attorney-in-fact signed her consent)? Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition, amendment, and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/28/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Notwithstanding the recitals on the first page of the fourth amendment, it is unusual for a trust to empower the surviving trustor to amend the dispositive terms of a trust after the first trustor's death. After procedural issues are addressed, an appearance will be required.)

rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for 04/20/2026.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LIANA M LYMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing to admit will. No appearance required. Status hearing on filing of petition for final distribution or petition for approval on report of status of administration will be set for 04/20/2026.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: LUCAS ANTHONY ARDUINI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF DEATH
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine whether the material on file provides sufficient grounds for the Court to establish the fact related to the death, including but not limited to the city/location of death being in San Francisco. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
